author: @SaCrEdKiD13

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: they should be - i would need a SEM to guarantee that - but size control is just a matter of reaction conditions - so concentration and temp being the two most important factors

replies.author: @SaCrEdKiD13
replies.text: +Robert Murray-Smith Hi Sir. Is there another simple way to hydrothermally carbonize the Fe nps? My vessel leaked and the solution spilled inside the furnace (I used a furnace 'coz the prof said I can't heat over 110 deg. C in the oven). I might not be able to use the furnace again because I cant remove the stain (another problem for me).

replies.text: about your only other option is a HTC autoclave - but they cost in the region of US$1,000 i am afraid

author: @trevorvanzuydam8115
text:
hi Rob, i wanted to make iron coated carbon, or carbon coated iron (to replace the graphene coated iron) for making a NiFe battery with KOH chemistry and nickel hydroxide. What i've done is dissolve sugar in FeCl and then added sulphuric acid. This then goes to a black colour eventually.
The problem i'm having is that i'm not a chemist and i dint know what i've created. is it just a mix of iron and carbon, or are they bonded ? Any thoughts?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Trevor Van Zuydam a mix of iron chloride, amorphous carbon and sulphuric acid - the acid is cataltic for the dehydration of sugars

replies.author: @trevorvanzuydam8115
replies.text: +Robert Murray-Smith so that would make it simply a mixture of iron and carbon. not really conducive, I guess I'll use the magnetite and go.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +Trevor Van Zuydam or you could hydrothermally carbonise it

replies.author: @trevorvanzuydam8115
replies.text: +Robert Murray-Smith I could, but I want to stick to a low energy approach.  like using just chems

author: @thethoughtemporium
text: Copper does weird things. It will cap it but if you change the catalyst out (use a teflon chamber and put a piece of copper in) it grows very sharp copper crystals but the optical properties are amazing. Literally sparkles. Oxidizes quickly so you need to get them into an inert environment quickly but otherwise very cool. I'll do a video on it soon

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +The Thought Emporium I am more than interested mate - go for it

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: I should qualify that previous post - it sits in suspension before the HTC treatment. After HTC the particles drop out of suspension quickly.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: after months it is still sitting in suspension. Interesting idea. I was wondering what it would do to the heat exchange properties of cooling fluids - added metal nanoparticles are known to improve this.

author: @JustinHallPlus
text: How well does the iron stay mixed?  Is it completely colloidal and non settling, or does it settle to the bottom of the vial after a few hours?  I could see something like this being used in a hydraulic transmission control system.

author: @alaasadek4125
text: Thanks robert. I will try it and let you know.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Keep it very dilute or add a dispersant. Windscreen washer fluid has non ionic dispersants in it. There is a trade off. If you keep it dilute it will take a lot more time and energy to use it. If you use a dispersant you will need to anneal the film before use at about 350 degrees centigrade - hope this helps

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Sulphuric acid is 32% by weight concentration when it is used in batteries. You can get battery. Acid from an auto supplier or just get an old battery and drain out the solution. Filter it and test the specific gravity. The Internet will tell you how to work out the concentration of acid you have from that. Then adjust it with water to the correct concentration. In order to stop agglomeration you need to do one of two things. Either cont

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: it's all about the redox potential. So it'd definitely work for anything lower on the reactivity series (Ni, Au, Ag, Pt, Pd, Co, Mn, Mo, Cu etc) but Mg is much higher (about -3 whereas Fe is around -0.7 I think) so I don't know - well not without trying it - are you thinking hydrogen storage here - carbon capped Mg would be good for that.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: cheers ate - i have though about extending this too.

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Yep and for de-toxification of water and soil. Iron nanoparticles are good at removing oil, heavy metals and organic poisons from soil.

author: @Mr.Chatbot.
text: A novel route was used to make an air stable iron nanoparticles with an average size of 10 nm were deposited on the surface of preformed carbon spherules with the aid of power ultrasound. This coating was achieved by ultrasonic irradiation of a slurry of carbon spherules and Fe(CO)5 in diphenylmethane for 90 min under an argon atmosphere. The as-prepared amorphous material was annealed in argon at 700 °C, forming air-stable iron deposited on carbon spherules. Your Cooking tea is a breakthrough.
